SEARCH
Package com.keyin.ticketnestbackend.rest.event

Class EventController

java.lang.Object
com.keyin.ticketnestbackend.rest.event.EventController
@RestController @RequestMapping("/api/events") public class EventController extends Object
EventController is a REST controller that handles HTTP requests related to events. It provides endpoints for creating, retrieving, updating, and deleting events. The controller uses the EventService to perform the necessary business logic and interacts with the EventRepository for data persistence. The endpoints are secured with role-based access control, allowing only users with the ADMIN role to create, update, or delete events, while any authenticated user can retrieve event information.

    • EventController

      public EventController()

  • Method Details

    • createEvent

      @PostMapping @PreAuthorize("hasRole(\'ADMIN\')") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<Event> createEvent(@Valid @RequestBody @Valid Event event)
      Endpoint to create a new event. This endpoint is secured and can only be accessed by users with the ADMIN role.
      Parameters:
      event - the event object to be created, which is validated using @Valid to ensure that it meets the defined constraints.
      Returns:
      a ResponseEntity containing the created event and an HTTP status of CREATED (201) if successful, or an error message if the event already exists or if validation fails.

    • getEventById

      @GetMapping("/{id}") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<Event> getEventById(@PathVariable Long id)
      Endpoint to retrieve an event by its ID. This endpoint can be accessed by any authenticated user. It takes the event ID as a path variable and returns the corresponding event if found. If the event is not found, it throws an IllegalArgumentException with a message indicating that the event was not found.
      Parameters:
      id - the ID of the event to retrieve, which is passed as a path variable in the URL.
      Returns:
      a ResponseEntity containing the retrieved event and an HTTP status of OK (200) if successful, or an error message if the event is not found.

    • getAllEvents

      @GetMapping public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<?> getAllEvents()
      Endpoint to retrieve all events. This endpoint can be accessed by any authenticated user. It returns a list of all events available in the system. The response is wrapped in a ResponseEntity with an HTTP status of OK (200). If there are no events, it returns an empty list.
      Returns:
      a ResponseEntity containing a list of all events and an HTTP status of OK (200). If there are no events, it returns an empty list.

    • updateEvent

      @PutMapping("/{id}") @PreAuthorize("hasRole(\'ADMIN\')") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<Event> updateEvent(@PathVariable Long id, @Valid @RequestBody @Valid Event eventDetails)
      Endpoint to update an existing event. This endpoint is secured and can only be accessed by users with the ADMIN role. It takes the event ID as a path variable and the updated event details in the request body. The updated event details are validated using @Valid to ensure that they meet the defined constraints. If the event is successfully updated, it returns the updated event with an HTTP status of OK (200). If the event is not found, it throws an IllegalArgumentException with a message indicating that the event was not found. If the updated event details are invalid, it returns an error message with an appropriate HTTP status code.
      Parameters:
      id - the ID of the event to update, which is passed as a path variable in the URL.
      eventDetails - the updated event details, which are validated using @Valid.
      Returns:
      a ResponseEntity containing the updated event and an HTTP status of OK (200) if successful, or an error message if the event is not found or if the updated event details are invalid.

    • deleteEvent

      @DeleteMapping("/{id}") @PreAuthorize("hasRole(\'ADMIN\')") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<Void> deleteEvent(@PathVariable Long id)
      Endpoint to delete an event by its ID. This endpoint is secured and can only be accessed by users with the ADMIN role. It takes the event ID as a path variable and deletes the corresponding event if found. If the event is successfully deleted, it returns an HTTP status of NO_CONTENT (204). If the event is not found, it throws an IllegalArgumentException with a message indicating that the event was not found.
      Parameters:
      id - the ID of the event to delete, which is passed as a path variable in the URL.
      Returns:
      a ResponseEntity with an HTTP status of NO_CONTENT (204) if successful, or an error message if the event is not found.
